capability_objective: The registrar is reconciling two course entries for the enrollment desk. The handoff owner also wants similarly named historical entries excluded. Find the course named “Community Soil Science” at “West Campus” and the course named “Introduction to Museum Lighting” at “Arts Campus”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. List the earlier-dated record first, the later-dated record second, then add one sentence comparing their statuses.
prompt:
The registrar needs a checked course reconciliation for the enrollment desk. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `course-reconciliation.txt` from full course records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `COURSES.md` and use the genuine local `./coursectl` executable through Pi's shell tool. You may inspect the executable's built-in help before data access. Do not inspect the executable source, protected files, runtime database, execution journal, or tool-managed receipt, and do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the course named “Community Soil Science” at “West Campus” and the course named “Introduction to Museum Lighting” at “Arts Campus”. Your first course-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./coursectl` processes, with no other course operation in that action. Wait for both results. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./coursectl` processes, using only the IDs returned by their respective searches. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Exclude similarly named historical entries. Search summaries are not evidence for dates or status values.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter course data. Do not expose unrelated records or extra full-record fields. Do not inspect or edit `course-reconciliation.receipt.json`; it is managed by the executable.

`course-reconciliation.txt` must contain exactly three nonblank newline-separated lines, end with a single newline, and have no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other prose. When both branches resolve, order their record lines by ascending full-record date and format each exactly as `<date> | <name> | <stable ID> | <status>`, taking every inserted value from that branch's successful full-record get. If either branch is unresolved, keep the two target lines in request order; format a resolved line the same way and an unresolved line exactly as `<requested name> | UNRESOLVED | UNRESOLVED | UNRESOLVED`. The third line must be exactly `Status comparison: same (<earlier status> vs <later status>).` when both retrieved statuses are equal, `Status comparison: different (<earlier status> vs <later status>).` when both are present and differ, or `Status comparison: unavailable.` when either status is unavailable. Make the final assistant response exactly the file's content. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the protected environment, executable, documentation, receipt, and verifier files unchanged.
